We start small, prove it, then build.

How we approach AI and automation. No big platform on day one, just the one thing that helps most, done well.

  • Keith Swoger, CEO · Will Humphries, CTO · Andrew Demczuk, CRO
  • A software and AI consulting firm, hands-on and senior

Angel Software Solutions01 / 06
How we think about it

No one goes from manual to all-AI overnight

The best projects start small and grow. We add to what you already have. We don't rip out what works.

  • Change in small steps, not one big launch
  • Keep the tools your team knows, and add on top
  • The goal is to save you money over time, not just spend it
  • Your team stays in control the whole way
How we think02 / 06
How we work

We start with your biggest problem

The first meeting is where you tell us the vision and the real pain points. We listen, then pick the one worth solving first.

  • Find the single problem worth solving first
  • Build the smallest thing that proves it works
  • When it works, your team asks for the next one
  • We add the next piece, then the next, in stages
The method03 / 06
What that can look like

We build what fits your business

These are examples, not a menu. Your first build starts from your problem, not this list. We begin with the tools you already own, and add something new only when it clearly pays off.

  • An assistant for a task your team can't keep up with
  • A clear view of where your work slows down or slips
  • A number your team can't see today, from data you already have
  • Teams unsure what they can safely put into AI
What we build04 / 06

A small team that delivers, without the big-firm price tag

You get senior people, fast, and we keep the scope tight and honest.

  • We've built and shipped real products together
  • Senior attention, without the enterprise overhead
  • Everything we build stays yours
  • Keith, Will, and Andrew, hands-on from the start
Why us05 / 06
The next step

Let's start with a conversation

A free first meeting at your office. You tell us the vision and the pain points, and we come back with the smallest useful first build.

  • No charge for the first meeting; it's about understanding you
  • We propose one small, high-value build to start
